summary: During early stages like Vesicular and Colloidal, some mild reactions may happen but significant edema isn't present until more advanced Granular/Nodular stages begin showing robust immunological activity leading towards Calcified states wherein residual pathology doesn’t typically cause notable swelling anymore absent exceptional scenarios. The absence of prominent edema marks primarily the Calcified Phase indicating resolution nearing completion after initial aggressive host defense mechanisms have largely subsided resulting only minor lingering effects if any at all due to established fibrotic changes characteristic thereof compared other dynamic periods preceding it involving higher levels acute tissue injury requiring fluid accumulation as part physiological repair cascade triggered initially upon infection introduction followed by gradual diminution culminating into quiescent chronicity marked predominantly by structural alterations devoid major functional impairment indicative healing endpoint achieved signifying disease enters dormancy mode characterized low risk recurrence further complications including persistent cerebral oedema formation thereby making late static forms such as fully developed calcifications least likely scenario presenting clinically evident brain swells despite potential underlying latent risks continuing albeit silent nature rendering them less probable candidates exhibiting overt signs neurological distress manifest through increased intracranial pressure symptoms necessitating urgent intervention otherwise manageable conservatively monitoring asymptomatic individuals closely while providing supportive care measures aimed mitigating discomfort alleviating related sequelae ensuring overall well-being sustained long-term prognosis favorable outcomes achievable provided appropriate management strategies employed diligently throughout entire duration
